summary: Test suite covers core requirements well: 27+ unit tests validate cartesian product generation, include/exclude rules, feature flags, strategy settings, and size validation; workflow structure tests verify file existence, triggers, and actionlint compliance; act integration test runs full workflow. However, edge case coverage is sparse—no tests for empty arrays, missing required fields, special characters, or complex multi-feature interactions. Error handling is minimal (only maxSize exceeded tested; file I/O errors, invalid JSON, missing files only implicitly tested via act). Act integration tests are brittle, relying on string-matching output assertions ('MATRIX_ASSERT_BASIC_COUNT=4') that depend on workflow implementation details. Would catch core logic regressions and workflow structure changes, but gaps in edge cases and error scenarios reduce confidence in catching all failures.
